content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (like ICM and RND from Topic A) can be repurposed as a memory pruning and prioritization system for state-of-the-art active learning agents (from Topic B), where an agent’s innate curiosity signals which unlabeled experiences are worth retaining in long-term memory for future learning, rather than just which unlabeled data points to query next. This creates a fully autonomous closed-loop learning system that selects both what data to learn from and what memories to keep without external reward shaping.
